description: Crown & throat were deep chestnut-rufous with rufous becoming lighter as it extended down the flanks. The rest of underparts were creamy beige. Black mask extended across face well past both eyes to nape. Nape was creamy beige bisected by thin dark line. Eye & bill were dark; bill was thinner than Vireo but fairly thick for a warbler. Back was gray with dark vertical streaks. Wings were dark with two prominent white wing bars on each wing. Tail was dark & fairly short(about 1/3 body length).Behaviors: Bird sang during most of observation's duration, while slowly foraging mostly in scrub oak.Call: "See-see-see-see-seesa-see." Similar to A. Redstart but softer, clearer, and less burry. Song also similar to Blackpoll Warbler but a bit louder.Plumage: adult
